revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[fr:Iki-gokoku-jinja]][[ja:壱岐神社]] {{Infobox religious building |name=Iki Gokoku Shrine|native_name=壱岐護国神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Iki jinja, Nagasaki Haiden.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.811097|N|129.762653|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | caption = Honden | map_caption = | location = |deity={{ill|Emperor Kameyama|en|Emperor Kameyama|qid=Q360972|lt=Emperor Kameyama|1=Emperor Kameyama}} , {{ill|Emperor Go-Uda|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Uda|qq=}} , {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Suketoki|qq=}} | festival = | consecration_year =1898 | type = ''{{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku shrine|qq=}}''
(Formerly ''{{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=}}'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Iki|qid=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}} }} {{Nihongo|{{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|1=Iki Gokoku Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Iki_Gokoku_Shrine|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}|壱岐護国神社|Iki Gokoku Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|qid=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province|1=Iki Province}} {{ill|Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website|url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/jinja/|access-date=2024-03-28|website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website|language=en|text=Iki Shrine/Ikigokoku Shrine Shonioketokiko & 2 other gods 駐車場ありトイレあり四十二社巡り 264 Seto-ura, Ashibe-cho, Iki-shi 〒811-5461MAP Regarding Goshuin Reservations There are goshuin that have been written and placed at the shrine in advance. After visiting the shrine, please offer 300 yen and take one. ☎0920-45-1276 ※Depending on the availability of the priest, there are times when you may not be able to receive a goshuin. Thank you for your kind understanding.}}{{Cite web|title=【壱岐神社】アクセス・営業時間・料金情報 - じゃらんnet|url=https://www.jalan.net/kankou/spt_42423ag2130011542/|access-date=2024-03-28|website=www.jalan.net|language=ja}} It is also called Iki Shrine. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku shrine|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku Shrines|qq=}}, or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasukuni Shrine|qq=}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shōkonsha|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku Shrines|qq=}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} However it also worships other deities than war dead, hence sometimes being called just Iki Shrine. Although some of the deities of each shrine overlap with those of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasukuni Shrine|qq=}},{{Efn|Yasukuni Shrine also enshrines the war dead as Heroic Spirits and was renamed from Tokyo Shokonsha, but includes {{ill|Empire of Japan|Empire of the Empire of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empire of Japan|qq=}}, [[:en:Citizens|Citizens]], {{ill|Koreans|lt=Koreans|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koreans|qq=}}, {{ill|Taiwanese people|en|Taiwanese people|qid=Q707133|lt=Taiwanese|1=Taiwanese people}}, etc. The difference is that people from anywhere are eligible to be enshrined.}} the deities of each shrine are not separated from Yasukuni Shrine, and they perform their own rituals by inviting the souls of their own deities. However there are some exceptions, such as {{ill|Hida Gokoku Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hida Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} in Gifu Prefecture and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|1=Iki Gokoku Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Iki_Gokoku_Shrine|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} in Nagasaki Prefecture. It was rennovated in 2005.{{Cite web|title=壱岐神社|url=http://www.ikiweb.com/ikijinjya/|access-date=2024-03-28|website=www.ikiweb.com}} == History == {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shōni Suketoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Suketoki|qq=}} was a war hero against the mongols. He died in 1281. The stone mound called "Shounii-sama" (ショウニイさま) was recognized in 1898 (the 31st year of the Meiji era) as the grave of {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shōni Suketoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Suketoki|qq=}}, embodying the faith of local people who have passed down and protected it for over 600 years. 1944: Construction of the main hall 1948: Enshrinement festival for the deities 1952: Iki Gokoku Shrine Festival 1955: A man was enshrined who died in the war from illness. 1956: On November 8, a portion of the spirits of those from Iki County who died in war or from illness were enshrined at Yasukuni Shrine. Approval was granted to also call it Iki Gokoku Shrine. == Main Festivals == January 1: New Year's Festival February 11: National Foundation Day Festival April 12: Eve of the Grand Festival April 13: Grand Festival Third Sunday of April: Regular Grand Festival, Offering Ceremony, Tomb Festival May 27: Navy Memorial Service August 15: End of War Memorial Festival December 31: New Year's Eve Festival == See Also == * {{ill|Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Martyrs Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martyrs Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martial temple|qq=}} and '{{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|qid=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple|1=Wen Wu temple}}' * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eternal Spring Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Cultural Renaissance|qq=}} * {{ill|Ancestral shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancestral shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gallant Garden|qq=}} * {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|qq=}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arlington National Cemetery|qq=}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Valhalla|qq=}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Walhalla (memorial)|qq=}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eternal Spring Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The common end of myriad good deeds|qq=}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greek hero cult|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinjō Shrine|ja|新城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Komodahama Shrine|ja|小茂田浜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komodahama Shrine|1=Komodahama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17991450}} * {{ill|Genkō Historical Museum|ja|元寇史料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō Historical Museum|1=Genkō Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10889644}} * {{ill|Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|lt=Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|ja|沖縄県護国神社|1=Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Okinawa_Gokoku_Shrine|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * [[Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] * {{ill|Ame-no-Tanagao Shrine|ja|天手長男神社|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tanagao Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|qid=Q1052938|lt=Iki|1=Iki Province}} {{ill|Provinces of Japan|en|Provinces of Japan|qid=Q860290|lt=Province|1=Provinces of Japan}} [[Ichinomiya]]) * {{ill|Kō Shrine|ja|興神社|1=Kō Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kō_Shrine|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|qid=Q1052938|lt=Iki|1=Iki Province}} {{ill|Provinces of Japan|en|Provinces of Japan|qid=Q860290|lt=Province|1=Provinces of Japan}} [[Sōja shrine]]) == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Iki Shrines}} }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from link|Iki Gokoku Shrine}} {{everybodywiki link|Iki Gokoku Shrine|Iki Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:壱岐神社]] [[fr:Utilisateur:Immanuelle/Iki-gokoku-jinja]] {{Commons category|Iki-jinja (Nagasaki)}} Japan]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
